_comment: EXPERIMENT profile 'long-window' — overlays the default profile. Widens the recent-signal scoring window from the baseline 60 days to 120 days (Radar:ScoringWindowDays). HYPOTHESIS: the AI directional-filing extractor produces high-confidence (0.9-0.95) GuidanceChange signals only from quarterly earnings 8-Ks (item 2.02), which arrive ~every 90 days — so a 60-day window leaves each company AI-signal-free for ~1/3 of every quarter, collapsing its EvidenceConfidence back to the keyword-only ceiling (best conf 0.65) and its Opportunity to the structural keyword max (~52, below the Investigate gate of 60). A 120-day window keeps the last quarter's AI read alive until the next earnings release replaces it. The formula's RecencyFloor (0.5) already half-decays older in-window signals, so widening adds recall without over-weighting stale news. This is a PURE pipeline-knob change (ScoringOptions.Window is documented as a tunable knob, not a weight) — no formula/RuleSetVersion/weight change — but it DOES widen the scoring window input, so the effective config re-fingerprints and gets its own data/scoring-configs artifact + a fresh price-efficacy segment. NOTE: this does NOT raise the AI signal's Strength (still 6, = the keyword max) — that is not config-bindable and needs a code change; this experiment isolates the window effect alone. Output routes to data/experiments/long-window/ so baseline data/ is untouched and directly comparable.
